definition: DOGE will substantially underdeliver against announced targets, with achievable deficit reduction of $50-200B against $1-2T announced. 70% mandatory spending constraint plus defense protection leaves ~15% of budget addressable, and historical realization rates are 0.3-0.6x.
evidence: 70% mandatory spending constraint + defense protection leaves ~15% of budget
Historical realization rate of 0.3-0.6x for announced spending cuts
Political economy paradox: most federally dependent states ($1.30-$1.80 per tax dollar) are governing coalition's electoral base
DOGE actual execution targets workforce/programs, not zero-multiplier procurement reform
$236B GAO improper payments figure includes definitional errors, not recoverable waste
open_questions: What is the 12-18 month political feedback cycle from federal dependency to electoral consequences?
What fraction of GAO-identified improper payments is actually recoverable?
